verdict.reasoning: InsecureEtherVault.withdrawAll() performs an արտաքին ETH transfer with msg.sender.call{value: balance}() at line 24 before zeroing userBalances[msg.sender] at line 27. A malicious recipient can reenter withdrawAll() during the call while the balance is still non-zero and drain funds repeatedly, which is the classic checks-effects-interactions violation. Slither’s cross-function note is also valid because deposit() and getUserBalance(address) read/write the same stale storage, but the primary issue is the reentrancy in withdrawAll().
